course_title: CI/CD Pipeline in Practice — Automated Deployment to AWS EC2 with GitLab and Jenkins
description: A hands-on course where you build a complete CI/CD pipeline from scratch: code pushed to GitLab is automatically built, tested, containerized, and deployed to an AWS EC2 server via Jenkins and docker compose. A simple, language-agnostic web app serves as the example, but the core skills — writing a Dockerfile, a Jenkinsfile, and the deployment logic — transfer directly to your own projects.
summary: From writing your first Dockerfile to configuring a GitLab webhook, building CI stages in Jenkins, pushing images to AWS ECR, and automating EC2 deployment with rollback and secret management — one full cycle of shipping code automatically.
target_audience: Developers setting up a CI/CD pipeline for the first time (comfortable with Git and the terminal; want to automate deployments to a server like EC2)

lectures.title: The Big Picture — What a CI/CD Pipeline Is and What We'll Build
objectives: Understand the problems of manual deployment: human error, lack of reproducibility, and the recurring cost of repetitive steps
See how GitLab, Jenkins, a container image registry, and EC2 connect into a single automated flow
Get a clear picture of the finished pipeline you will have built by the end of this course

lectures.title: Containerizing Your App — Writing a Dockerfile and Running Locally
objectives: Understand why a container image is the preferred deployment artifact
Learn the basic pattern for containerizing a language-agnostic web app with a Dockerfile
Verify the container works locally with docker build and docker run

lectures.title: Setting Up GitLab and Triggering Jenkins on Push (Webhooks)
objectives: Understand the basic GitLab workflow — creating a repository, pushing code, and branching strategy
Learn how a GitLab webhook delivers push events to Jenkins
Configure the GitLab → Jenkins connection so a push automatically triggers the pipeline

lectures.title: Installing Jenkins and Your First Pipeline (Jenkinsfile)
objectives: Start Jenkins and create a pipeline job
Understand Pipeline as Code (Jenkinsfile) and the stage/step structure
Write a checkout stage that pulls source code from GitLab

lectures.title: The CI Stages — Build, Test, and Create the Image
objectives: Add build and test stages to the Jenkinsfile and ensure the pipeline stops on failure
Configure a stage that builds a Docker image after tests pass
Understand how CI automatically filters out low-quality code

lectures.title: Pushing the Image to a Registry (AWS ECR)
objectives: Understand the role of a container registry (ECR) and why built images are stored there
Handle ECR authentication from Jenkins and push the image
Define an image tagging strategy using commit SHA or similar identifiers

lectures.title: Automated Deployment to EC2 — SSH + docker compose
objectives: Securely connect from Jenkins to EC2 over SSH
Write a deployment stage that pulls the latest image and replaces the running container with docker compose
Complete the pipeline so that a single push deploys all the way to EC2

lectures.title: Health Checks, Rollback, and Managing Secrets
objectives: Use a health check to verify a successful deployment and roll back to the previous image on failure
Separate environment variables and secrets from source code and inject them safely
Review the completed pipeline and identify how to adapt it to your own application
